name: Masmut Rocks Loop Trail
name_original: Vuelta a las Peñas del Masmut (PR-TE 157)
description: Hike the difficult 10.3-mile Return to the Peñas del Masmut (PR-TE 157) for stunning rock formations and panoramic views.
region_intro: Hiking around Peñarroya De Tastavins offers exploration of the Matarraña region's rugged natural landscapes. The area is characterized by the Puertos de Beceite mountain range, featuring prominent conglomerate monoliths like the Peñas del Masmut and deep ravines. Pine forests and the Tastavins River valley contribute to the diverse terrain, providing varied environments for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Peñarroya De Tastavins The most popular hiking route is Masmut Rocks Loop Trail, a difficult 10.3 miles (16.6 km) trail that takes 5 hours 5 minutes to complete. This route offers spectacular rock formations and panoramic views of the Masmut Rocks. Another top favourite among local hikers is View of the Masmut Cliffs – Peñas del Masmut Trail loop from Peñarroya de Tastavins / Pena-roja de Tastavins, a moderate 8.0 miles (12.9 km) path. It provides impressive views of the conglomerate monoliths and the surrounding landscape. Local hikers also love the Pantano de Pena – Pena Reservoir loop from La Rica, a 5.2 miles (8.3 km) trail leading through pine forests with views of the Ulldecona Reservoir, often completed in about 2 hours 15 minutes. Hiking around Peñarroya De Tastavins is defined by the Puertos de Beceite mountain range, the unique Masmut Rocks, and the Tastavins River valley. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy paths to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Peñarroya De Tastavins are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 300 reviews. More than 2800 hikers have used komoot to explore Peñarroya De Tastavins's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Peñarroya De Tastavins?
A: There are over 130 hiking trails in Peñarroya De Tastavins, offering a wide range of options for all skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes over 2800 times, highlighting the area's popularity for outdoor enthusiasts.

Q: What are the different difficulty levels of hiking trails in Peñarroya De Tastavins?
A: The trails around Peñarroya De Tastavins cater to various fitness levels. You'll find 31 easy routes, 77 moderate paths, and 24 more challenging trails, ensuring there's something for everyone, from leisurely strolls to demanding mountain ascents.

Q: What is the best season to hike in Peñarroya De Tastavins?
A: The region's Mediterranean climate makes spring and autumn ideal for hiking, with pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended. Winter offers a unique, tranquil experience, though some higher routes might require more preparation.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Peñarroya De Tastavins?
A: Yes, many trails in Peñarroya De Tastavins are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10419019" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Masmut Cliffs – Peñas del Masmut Trail loop&lt;/a&gt; offers impressive views of the iconic conglomerate monoliths, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6930196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Masmut – Viewpoint of Masmut loop&lt;/a&gt; provides a shorter, scenic option.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in Peñarroya De Tastavins?
A: Many trails in the Peñarroya De Tastavins area are dog-friendly, allowing you to explore the natural beauty with your canine companion. It's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock, and to check specific trail regulations beforehand.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Peñarroya De Tastavins?
A: Absolutely. The region offers several easier routes suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981174546/hermitage-of-san-miguel-de-fuentespalda-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Miguel de Fuentespalda Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.17 km path with panoramic views, perfect for a family outing. The botanical garden, Flora, The Garden of Natural Life, also provides an engaging and accessible experience.

Q: Where can I find impressive viewpoints or natural landmarks during a hike?
A: The Peñas del Masmut are the most iconic natural feature, offering stunning rock formations and panoramic vistas. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981158803/masmut-rocks-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Masmut Rocks Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; provide spectacular views. You can also spot vultures nesting in this area. The Ports of Beceite mountain range itself is full of dramatic landscapes and peaks.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls or gorges?
A: Yes, the Matarraña region is known for its beautiful ravines and water features. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4978626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parrissal Walkways&lt;/a&gt;, a route with wooden structures navigating gorges. For waterfalls, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2479086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de Robert Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2882512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portellada Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, both offering refreshing natural sights.

Q: What cultural or historical sites can I visit while hiking in the area?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can visit the Santuario de la Virgen de la Fuente, located near the Tastavins River. This complex includes hermitages and a guesthouse, with the 'Ermita de Dalt' featuring a remarkable 14th-century Gothic-Mudejar wooden ceiling that is a UNESCO World Heritage site.

Q: Is there parking available for hikers in Peñarroya De Tastavins?
A: Yes, Peñarroya De Tastavins and the surrounding trailheads typically offer designated parking areas for hikers. It's advisable to check specific starting points for larger trails, such as those leading to the Masmut Rocks, for detailed parking information.

Q: How can I reach the hiking trails in Peñarroya De Tastavins by public transport?
A: While Peñarroya De Tastavins is a rural village, limited public transport options may connect it to larger towns in the Teruel province. However, for direct access to many trailheads, especially those deeper in the Puertos de Beceite, a car is generally recommended for flexibility.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Peñarroya De Tastavins?
A: The trails in Peñarroya De Tastavins are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 300 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the spectacular rock formations of the Masmut, the diverse landscapes of the Puertos de Beceite, and the tranquility of the pine forests and river valleys.
tips: This entertaining and not so demanding path goes around the wonderful rock formation known as Peñas del Masmut. Throughout the entire journey you will have beautiful views that will be even more impressive if you decide to reach the top of the massif where the viewpoint is located.
From here we have the best view of the Masmut rocks. An impressive landscape.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Return to the Peñas del Masmut (PR-TE 157) trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is located in Peñarroya de Tastavins, Spain. You can typically find parking in or near the village, which serves as the starting point for this circular route. Specific trailhead parking information might be available locally upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is the Return to the Peñas del Masmut (PR-TE 157) trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself doesn't explicitly prohibit dogs, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife like the important vulture colony. Ensure your dog is capable of handling the trail's moderate to high difficulty and stony sections.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best times to hike this route are generally spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. It's advisable to avoid periods of heavy rain, as the Arroyo de los Prados (Meadows Stream) can swell, making some sections challenging. Summer can be very hot, so an early start is recommended if hiking during that season.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route or the Puertos de Beceite natural area?
faqs.answer: The Return to the Peñas del Masmut (PR-TE 157) trail is located within the natural area of Els Ports de Tortosa-Beceite. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this particular trail. However, it's always good practice to check for any local regulations or advisories before your visit.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain consists primarily of a mix of tracks and paths. Be prepared for some stony sections, especially as you circle the rocky massif. You'll traverse diverse landscapes, including forested rolling hills and deep ravines, with a descent to the Arroyo de los Prados. Appropriate mountain footwear is essential.

faqs.question: What notable features or wildlife can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: The main highlight is the spectacular reddish rock formations of the Peñas del Masmut. You might also spot an important colony of vultures soaring overhead. The trail features ancient farmhouses, small rock shelters, and the View of the Masmut Cliffs, offering panoramic views. You'll also pass the Peñas del Masmut Trail itself.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally described as having a moderate to high level of difficulty, with some sources calling it 'difficult'. Given its distance of over 16 km, significant elevation gain, and stony paths, it is not typically recommended for beginners. Hikers should have a good level of physical fitness and appropriate gear.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route shares sections with several other paths. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Vuelta a las Peñas de Masmut, Pedals dels Ports, Senda del Maestrazgo, and Los Prados, among others. This can offer opportunities for longer or alternative explorations if you're familiar with the area.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints along the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, there are several points offering impressive views. The "Mirador de les Roques" is particularly notable, providing some of the best panoramic vistas of the Masmut rock formations and the surrounding Matarraña region. The View of the Masmut Cliffs is also a key spot.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and terrain, you should bring appropriate mountain footwear with good ankle support. Carry plenty of water, especially during warmer months, as there might not be reliable water sources along the entire route. Sun protection, snacks, and a map or GPS device are also highly recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any historical or cultural elements to see on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail offers glimpses into the region's history. You'll pass by small rock shelters and constructions built into the rock, historically used for livestock. The route also follows part of the "Camino de Vallibona," a path used for a septennial pilgrimage, adding a cultural layer to your hike.
